plan: Reduce all leveraged ETFs to zero to cut risk.
Trim income and broad index exposure to free up capital.
Significantly reduce SPY to address concentration and create buffer.
Add AMAT, a high-rated semiconductor name, for growth.
Add XLV, a defensive healthcare ETF, for stability.
This plan prioritizes capital preservation with a cash buffer.
conservative.read: This plan aims for ~1.5% monthly return by cutting leverage and focusing on quality. Expect lower volatility and drawdowns.

plan: Reduce leveraged ETFs to a minimal level for risk control.
Trim income and broad index holdings to reduce concentration.
Significantly reduce SPY to meet the concentration mandate.
Add AMAT, a high-rated semiconductor name, for growth.
Add RTX, a strong industrial name, for diversification.
This plan balances growth with risk reduction and a moderate cash buffer.
balanced.read: This plan targets ~2.5% monthly return by reducing leverage and concentration. It blends quality tech with broader market exposure, aiming for risk-first growth.

plan: Increase leveraged ETFs (SOXL, SPAL) for amplified growth potential.
Sell income holdings to reallocate capital to higher-growth areas.
Reduce broad index exposure to concentrate on high-conviction names.
Significantly reduce SPY to address concentration and fund adds.
Add AMAT and RTX, strong scanner setups, for concentrated growth.
This plan accepts higher risk and drawdown for aggressive growth.
aggressive.read: This plan targets ~4% monthly return by increasing leverage and concentrating in top-rated setups. Expect higher volatility, significant drawdowns, and daily decay from leveraged ETFs.

plan: Eliminate all leveraged ETFs and high-beta tech holdings.
Significantly increase QQQI to maximize income generation.
Maintain SPY and QQQ for broad market exposure and stability.
Add XLV for defensive sector exposure and income potential.
This plan prioritizes steady monthly distributions over growth.
Focus is on capital preservation with reliable income.
income.read: This plan aims for ~1% monthly return by focusing on income-generating assets. Expect low volatility and reliable distributions, with NAV potentially falling in market downturns.
